_comment: Curated measure→issue mappings for the Voting Record 'stance vs. record' verdict. Applied by applyCuratedIssueSeed() in netlify/lib/vr-ingest.ts. Each entry attaches to a measure ONLY when a real measure with the same (measureType, congress, chamber, canonical number) already exists — created by the seed migration or by a live Congress.gov ingest. Entries that don't match anything yet are harmless no-ops until that roll call is ingested, so nothing here can invent a measure. supportMeaning is binary: 'yea_supports' (a yea advances the issue) or 'yea_opposes' (a yea cuts against it) — see _voteEffectiveSupport in stance-helpers.js.

issues.rationale: Extends and expands the 2017 individual and business tax cuts.
Enacts net spending reductions through budget reconciliation.
Reduces federal Medicaid spending and tightens eligibility; a yea vote cuts against expanding healthcare access.
Provides new funding for border enforcement and immigration operations.
Expands onshore and offshore oil, gas, and coal leasing on federal land.
Mandates detention and removal proceedings for covered unauthorized immigrants.
Tightens immigration enforcement.
SAVE Act — requires documentary proof of U.S. citizenship to register to vote in federal elections.
Adds a citizenship-documentation requirement to voter registration.
Critics hold the documentation requirement narrows ballot access; a yea vote cuts against expanding voting access.
Sets the fiscal blueprint and reconciliation instructions that framed the year's tax-and-spending changes.
Directs committees to find net spending reductions through reconciliation.
Reserves reconciliation room to extend and expand the 2017 tax cuts.
Congressional Review Act resolution repealing the IRS reporting rule for decentralized-finance brokers; a yea rolls back the mandate.
Supporters framed the rule as unworkable for decentralized software and a drag on U.S. crypto development.
Reasserts congressional authority over tariffs by ending the emergency used to impose them; a yea rolls back the executive tariff action.
Removes the duties on Canadian imports; a yea favors lower trade barriers with a top trading partner.
Supporters argued the tariffs raise costs for U.S. consumers and businesses; a yea removes them.
Requires congressional authorization before military action against Iran; a yea favors restraint and a congressional check on war-making.
A yea presses against open-ended involvement in a new Middle East conflict.
Opponents held that constraining the commander-in-chief would weaken deterrence; a yea cuts against that view.
Exempts qualified tip income from federal income tax for tipped workers.
Aims to raise take-home pay for service and hospitality workers.
Reduces the federal tax burden on a category of earned income.
Repeals the waiver behind California's EV sales mandate; a yea rolls back a major state climate rule.
Supporters framed it as protecting consumer choice and gasoline-vehicle access.
Uses the Congressional Review Act to strike a federal waiver/rule.
Repeals the waiver behind California's zero-emission truck mandate; a yea rolls back a major state climate rule.
Supporters framed it as easing costs on trucking and freight.
Uses the Congressional Review Act to strike a federal waiver/rule.
